mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS while CPIAUCSL remains elevated signals easing policy relative to inflation, which lowers short-rate expectations and supports higher 10-year Treasury yields over the next month as investors price less aggressive tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS falls while CPIAUCSL is stable or rising and the 10-year yield still declines in a majority of 20-day windows, or if the sign of the yield response is negative in more than 60% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above its recent 3-month trend, growth and inflation expectations weaken, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as markets price slower nominal activity.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day yield changes in more than 60% of cases or if the average yield change is not negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer demand and housing activity, which lifts nominal growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in retail sales and housing starts do not correspond to positive 20-day yield changes more than half the time, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rebound in INDPRO from a low base signals improved capacity utilization and stronger real activity, which raises term yields over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations reprice quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO surprises are not followed by positive 5-day yield changes in at least 55% of observations, or if the average 5-day response is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains firm while FEDFUNDS is still near the effective lower bound, real-rate expectations are not anchored by policy easing, so the 10-year yield rises over the next 20 trading days as inflation persistence dominates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL is flat or increasing over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if low policy rates and persistent CPI do not produce positive 20-day yield changes more often than not, or if the response disappears once CPI is rising but yields still fall.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series after prior gains marks labor-market softening and weaker future income growth, which depresses long-rate expectations and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll declines are not followed by negative 60-day yield changes in a majority of cases or if the signal flips positive after controlling for revisions.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration, which reduces inflation pressure and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts on the latest quarter while RSAFS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contraction plus weaker retail sales fail to precede negative 20-day yield changes in at least 60% of cases, or if the combined signal is weaker than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening or rebound regime, rising HOUST together with improving INDPRO signals stronger supply-side recovery and higher real-rate expectations, which lifts the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-shock rebounds in housing starts and industrial production do not produce positive 60-day yield changes in a majority of cases, or if the effect is absent outside recovery periods.
